task_completion.description: The target image is opened in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The target image (a colorful landscape or a blue-red swirl gradient) is visible in the GIMP workspace.

task_completion.description: The Posterize dialog box is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Posterize' dialog window (typically accessed via Colors > Posterize) is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The posterize levels are configured to 4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Posterize dialog, the 'Posterize levels' input field is set to exactly 4.

task_completion.description: The posterize effect is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas exhibits a poster-like appearance with distinct color banding and flat color areas (lack of smooth gradients). The Posterize dialog should be confirmed (closed) in the final state.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with GIMP's menus and dialogs rather than executing terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to modify the image.

integrity.description: The effect was applied using the actual Posterize tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the built-in Posterize tool rather than manually painting over the image or using unrelated filters to fake the appearance.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is a colorful landscape (or a blue-red swirl gradient if the fallback was used). The task requires using GIMP's Posterize effect. The Posterize levels must be set to exactly 4. When applied, the image will have a theoretical maximum of 64 unique colors, resulting in characteristic color banding where continuous color gradients are reduced to discrete, flat color areas.
